name: Lady Sun ([Sun Shangxiang])
page_title: Lady Sun ([Sun Shangxiang]) 孫妃 ([孫尚香]) [Wu, Shu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Lady Sun’s interest in returning to Wu was not well-intentioned. She stole away with Liu Shan after meeting with a Wu messenger. Though Liu Shan was recovered, she never returned.
	Lady Sun did not commit suicide in AD 221 upon hearing of Liu Bei’s defeat at Yiling.

sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	孫妃 ([孫尚香])


	 Simplified Chinese 
	孙妃[孙尚香]


	 Pronunciation 
	Sun1 Fei1 ([sun1shang4xiang1]) 


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Sūn Fēi ([sūnshàngxiāng])


	 Wade-Giles 
	Lady Sun Sun Shanghsiang

	 Birthplace 
	Fuchun County, Wu Prefecture (Presently Fuyang, Zhejiang Province)

	 Other Names 
	Sun Shangxiang, Sun Ren (孫仁), Sun Li, SSX

	 Name Notes 
	Sun Shangxiang is Lady Sun’s name in most Koei Games. ‘Sun Ren’ (孫仁) in the seventh chapter of Romance of the Three Kingdoms (learn more; not a duplicate mention of Sun Lang). Also appears as Sun Li at times. Both Sun Ren and Sun Li are incorrect and have no bearing on her true name, which is unknown.

full_text:
Lady Sun ([Sun Shangxiang]) 孫妃 ([孫尚香])
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Lady Sun (孫妃), styled[Sun Shangxiang] ([孫尚香]), was born in Fuchun County, Wu Prefecture (Presently Fuyang, Zhejiang Province). Sun Quan’s sister. Married to Liu Bei by Sun Quan, but later returned to Wu. Lady Sun was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom and the Shu Kingdom. Lady Sun lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
Lady Sun’s interest in returning to Wu was not well-intentioned. She stole away with Liu Shan after meeting with a Wu messenger. Though Liu Shan was recovered, she never returned.
Lady Sun did not commit suicide in AD 221 upon hearing of Liu Bei’s defeat at Yiling.
